old_claim.note: Plant Domestication provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct plant_domestication edge remains because papyrus writing surfaces require identifying, harvesting, and processing papyrus stems, not evidence that papyrus had to be domesticated.

source_shape.source_locator: Metropolitan Museum essay explains papyrus manufacture through stripping the green rind from papyrus stems and cutting the pith into strips, without identifying plant domestication as a requirement.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports plant identification and material processing for papyrus sheets, not domestication of papyrus.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Plant domestication is too strong and specific for this edge; foraging_and_botany captures the required plant knowledge without adding an unsupported agricultural claim.

ontology_before: Papyrus & Parchment Writing Surfaces directly depended on Plant Domestication, implying papyrus sheets required domesticated plant production.
ontology_after: Papyrus & Parchment Writing Surfaces depends on foraging_and_botany for the papyrus branch and avoids claiming domestication where the source only supports harvesting and processing papyrus stems.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: plant_domestication is no longer a direct prerequisite for paper_making_papyrus_parchment.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a later agricultural papyrus-cultivation industry rather than early papyrus writing surfaces.
A stronger source showed early papyrus sheet production depended specifically on domesticated papyrus plants.
A separate papyrus-cultivation node were added and should carry a plant_domestication prerequisite.
short_reason: Papyrus-sheet evidence supports plant gathering and processing, not plant domestication.
